title: mqobsidian inbox-manifest.v1
description: Canonical exported view of the promotion inbox: candidate memories that have been observed but not yet promoted to durable memory. mqobsidian owns this contract under the single-source-of-truth model (ROADMAP Delivery A/D; see DEC-002). This manifest is the current-state surface consumers read; the raw inbox itself (vault inbox/ folder) is private and gitignored. Item state uses the PRE-PROMOTION end of the canonical promotion axis (observed/candidate) shared with memory-score.v1 / promotion-event.v1; anything past 'candidate' has left the inbox. Ranking and the review-needed-vs-auto-promotable decision are mq-agent orchestration concerns (ROADMAP Delivery B), NOT encoded as inbox state here — this manifest carries the score input, not the routing verdict.

schema.description: Schema discriminator. Consumers must match this exact value before trusting the shape.

source.description: Producer identity that generated this manifest. mqobsidian is the sole producer.

generated_at.description: When this manifest was materialized. Freshness marker for staleness thresholds (Delivery D).

items.description: Current inbox contents. Each item is a candidate memory awaiting a promotion decision; the array is the whole current inbox surface.

id.description: Stable inbox-item identifier. Traceable to the durable note if this item is later promoted (promotion-event.v1.memory_id).

source.description: Where the candidate originated (e.g. a repo key or ingestion channel). Not a file path.

evidence.description: Pointers to the source evidence supporting this candidate — the traceability chain the exit criteria require (every promoted item traces to source evidence).

ref.description: Reference to a source record/event (e.g. a memory-observation.v1 id or vault-relative note path). Never absolute/machine-specific.

kind.description: Optional evidence kind (e.g. observation, feedback, review) for display/filtering only.

first_seen.description: When this candidate first entered the inbox. With last_seen/occurrences it forms the recurrence signal ranking consumes (Delivery B).

last_seen.description: Most recent time this candidate was observed.

occurrences.description: How many times the candidate has been observed. A ranking INPUT only; frequency never promotes on its own (see memory-score.v1 / ADR-008).

state.description: Pre-promotion position on the canonical promotion axis. 'observed' = seen; 'candidate' = accumulating evidence toward review. Promotion beyond this removes the item from the inbox and is recorded as a promotion-event.v1.

score.description: Optional current ranking score mirrored from memory-score.v1 for this candidate. Provided as a read convenience; memory-score.v1 remains the authority for how it is computed.
